channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Major Russian attack on Ukraine kills four, wounds dozens
item.description: Russia launched a major attack on Ukraine, killing four and wounding dozens with drones and missiles. Russia has been launching smaller ⁠barrages of dozens of drones every night at Ukraine, interspersing them ‌with occasional large-scale attacks that use hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles.

item.title: Ireland to demand Israel pay for settler demolition of Palestinian school
item.description: Ireland will demand Israel repay the cost of a demolished Palestinian school. Ireland will demand that Israeli authorities repay the cost of a school that was demolished by settlers. Ireland to demand Israel pay for settler demolition of Palestinian school.

item.title: No clarity on U.S.-Iran talks as Islamabad stays under lockdown
item.description: Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister expressed hope for meaningful engagements between the US and Iran to promote regional peace and stability. Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Dar, in a social media post, expressed hope for "meaningful engagements" between the two warring parties to promote regional peace and stability.

item.title: 2nd Round Of Talks In Pakistan Today, Commercial Flights Resume In Tehran
item.description: Commercial flights resumed in Tehran after talks. US-Iran War News Live Update:. 2nd Round Of Talks In Pakistan Today, Commercial Flights Resume In Tehran.

item.title: Romania says drone fragments damage property during overnight Russian attack on Ukraine
item.description: Romania's Defence Ministry condemned Russia's overnight attack on Ukraine, which damaged property with drone fragments, posing a new challenge to regional security. The Defence Ministry firmly condemns the irresponsible actions of the Russian Federation and emphasises that these represent ​a new challenge ‌to regional security and stability in the Black Sea area.

item.title: US says two killed in boat strike as toll climbs over 180
item.description: The US military conducted a strike on a vessel, killing two, with a toll over 180. The military "conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations", the US Southern Command said.

item.title: Ukraine sacks top commander after ‘starving’ soldiers’ rib-showing photos go viral
item.description: Ukraine sacked its top commander after photos of starving soldiers went viral. Ukraine sacks top commander after ‘starving’ soldiers’ rib-showing photos go viral.

item.title: The 85-Year-Old Widow Snagged by Trump’s Immigration Crackdown
item.description: Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, an 85-year-old French widow of a former G. I., was deported from the US under Trump's immigration crackdown. In her first interview since being deported, she recounted her experience in ICE detention.

item.title: Iran's foreign minister awaits U.S. delegation to Pakistan for peace talks
item.description: Iran's Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad for peace talks, awaiting a US delegation including Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. Iran's Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad on Friday, as the White House confirmed Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal.".

item.title: Capture of ships by US, Iran violates international law, shipping body says
item.description: The International Chamber of Shipping director said the US and Iran should release captured vessel crews, as their actions violate international law. US and Iran should immediately release crews of captured vessels, says International Chamber of Shipping director.

item.title: US ‘punishment’ plan for allies deepens Europe rift; Nato says expulsion not possible
item.description: The US plans to punish its allies, deepening the rift with Europe. Nato says expulsion is not possible, but the move has sparked concern among member countries. US ‘punishment’ plan for allies deepens Europe rift; Nato says expulsion not possible.

item.title: What is Nato and which countries are in it?
item.description: Nato is a military alliance formed in 1949, comprising 30 countries including the US, UK, and Canada. Its primary goal is to provide collective defense against potential security threats. What is Nato and which countries are in it?

item.title: US Mistakenly Puts Australian Flags Near White House For King Charles' Visit
item.description: The US mistakenly displayed Australian flags near the White House ahead of King Charles' visit. Charles is the head of state for Australia, but the role is largely ceremonial. Charles is also the head of state for Australia, but the role is largely ceremonial.

item.title: US, Iran To Hold 2nd Round Of Talks In Pakistan Today. What's On Agenda
item.description: Iran and the US will hold a second round of peace talks in Pakistan. The previous round occurred on April 11-12, amid a delicate ceasefire. The previous round of talks was held on April 11-12.

item.title: Melania Trump Is Growing White House Honey Program With New Beehive
item.description: Melania Trump has expanded the White House honey program with a new beehive, funded by the Trust for the National Mall. The new hive aims to promote bee conservation and honey production on the White House grounds.

item.title: Japan’s New Care Workers: Bodybuilders, Wrestlers and M.M.A. Fighters
item.description: Japan recruits bodybuilders and athletes to work in nursing homes due to staff shortages. Young athletes are being recruited to plug a staff shortage in Japan’s nursing homes. The results are reshaping elder care.

item.title: Iran's foreign minister arrives in Pakistan, Trump expects offer satisfying US demands
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian arrived in Pakistan. The White House announced special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel to Pakistan for talks. Iran says it has no plans to meet US officials, while the White House says special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel to Pakistan on Saturday (Apr 25) for talks.

item.title: What’s happening on day 57 as Trump dispatches negotiating team?
item.description: US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel to Pakistan for Iran peace talks, as Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad. The US negotiating team's visit aims to revive stalled talks.

item.title: US military kills two more people in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific
item.description: The US military killed two people in a strike on a boat in the eastern Pacific.

item.title: US State Dept orders global warning about alleged AI thefts by DeepSeek, other Chinese firms
item.description: The US State Department issued a global warning about alleged AI thefts by Chinese firms. US State Dept orders global warning about alleged AI thefts by DeepSeek, other Chinese firms.
